title: Bank Balance Growth
description: Interest earned from a balance is added back into the balance. Useful for compound growth and delayed financial consequences.

seed_text.systembrief: Interest earned from a balance is added back into the balance. Useful for compound growth and delayed financial consequences.
seed_text.studentdecision: Students choose the student-controlled levers and observe how the system changes over time.
seed_text.learninggoal: Students should explain the behaviour-over-time pattern and identify which decisions improved or harmed the system.
seed_text.riskychoice: Push the most tempting short-term lever without considering delayed or system-wide effects.
seed_text.safechoice: Balance short-term gains with longer-term system health and capacity.

title: Optimum Point: Trench Team Size
description: Too few workers are slow; too many workers create coordination loss. Useful for showing that more input is not always better.

title: Boom and Collapse: Public Interest
description: Public interest grows through sharing, but fatigue builds until the system collapses. Useful for overshoot-and-collapse discussions.

title: Oscillation: Seasonal Public Interest
description: Public interest rises and falls with a repeating seasonal push while fading over time. Useful for demand, attention, workload, and seasonal cycles.

title: Success to the Successful
description: Small early advantages compound because success attracts more future success. Useful for inequality, market dominance, and resource allocation.
primary_stock: Group A Success / Group B Success
